_meta.description: Wave 3 process-object governance policy for extracting missing lifecycle owners and blocking duplicate process objects.

objective.name: Process Object Introduction And Duplicate Suppression
objective.statement: Each process object must exist only when it closes a real operational blind spot. If the process object already exists as a governed lifecycle owner, the platform must not create a duplicate. If the object is still embedded inside another lifecycle owner and that embedding hides demand, gate, KPI, or audit truth, Wave 3 must extract it into a first-class canonical object.

build_questions.question: What business commitment, gate, KPI, or audit trail is this process object born to protect, and what operational blind spot remains if it stays embedded?
Does this object already exist as a governed lifecycle owner elsewhere in runtime, making a new table or endpoint a harmful duplicate?
If the object is embedded in a parent record today, what exact fields and lifecycle transitions must be extracted so the parent stops acting as a split truth container?
What gate is enforced by this object, and can that gate be defended without a dedicated lifecycle owner?
If a candidate object is only an alias today, what objective criteria would justify separating it later, and until then what canonical successor remains active?
Can the extracted object trace cleanly to quote, sales order, production, shipping, or inspection records without dual ownership?
Do create and transition actions for the extracted object replay safely under retry and preserve status history?
If the candidate object adds no distinct gate or KPI, has it been explicitly classified as duplicate, alias, unused, or archive instead of left ambiguous?
